definition: A careful examination of a product, machine or process to check its condition or quality.
examples: The operator performed a visual inspection before starting the production line to ensure that all parts were correctly positioned.
The quality inspection found a small crack on the welded part.
After the inspection was completed, production continued without any problems.

definition: Work performed to keep equipment in good condition and prevent or repair failures.
examples: The maintenance team replaced the damaged proximity sensor and tested the machine before restarting production.
Preventive maintenance helps reduce unexpected machine failures.
The robot was stopped for scheduled maintenance during the night shift.

definition: The amount of light produced by or reflected from a surface, screen or image.
examples: The camera brightness was increased to make the part easier to detect.
Incorrect brightness settings can cause unreliable inspection results.
The operator adjusted the screen brightness before checking the image.

definition: The straight-line distance across the center of a circle or cylindrical object.
examples: The operator measured the diameter of the shaft with a digital caliper.
The hole diameter must remain within the specified tolerance.
A larger pipe diameter allows more material to flow through the system.
